DECLARATION OF ANDREA D. MCGARY, ESQ RE SERVICE OF PLAINTIFFS OPPOSITION TO DEMURRER OF DEFENDANT LABAHN RYAN ARCHITECTS (TRANSACTION ID # 100025880) FILED BY PLAINTIFF O'LEARY, TESSA AN INDIVIDUAL O'LEARY, COLMAN AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LABAHN RYAN ARCHITECTS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, AS TO ALL FIVE CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFFS TESSA AND COLEMAN O'LEARY IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR COMMON COUNTS AND OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, THIRD AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE O'LEARYS ADEQUATELY ALLEGE CLAIMS FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, FRAUD, CONSPIRACY TO COMMIT FRAUD AND VIOLATION OF THE UNFAIR COMPETITION LAW. THE COMMON COUNTS CLAIM IS INADEQUATELY ALLEGED. EVEN WITH LIBERAL CONSTRUCTION, THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT ALLEGE THE REASON THAT DEFENDANTS BECAME INDEBTED TO THE O'LEARYS OTHER THAN FOR THE COSTS INCURRED BY THE O'LEARYS TO REMEDIATE DEFENDANTS' ASSERTEDLY SUBSTANDARD WORK. WHILE THOSE COSTS ARE RECOVERABLE AS CONTRACT DAMAGES, THEY DO NOT SUPPORT A COMMON COUNT CLAIM. IF THE O'LEARY CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, THEY ARE GIVEN LEAVE TO ALLEGE A COGNIZABLE COMMON COUNT CLAIM. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR NO. 6576, E-MAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-15-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Andrea D. McGary of the law offices of The McGary Firm (415-874-3500) appears by telephone as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Owen J. Rescher of the law offices of Rescher Law PC (415-722-8923) appears by telephone as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT LABAHN RYAN ARCHITECTS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, as to all five causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiffs Tessa and Coleman O'Leary is SUSTAINED with twenty days leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action for common counts and overruled as to the first, second, third and fifth causes of action. The O'Learys adequately allege claims for breach of contract, fraud, conspiracy to commit fraud and violation of the Unfair Competition Law. The common counts claim is inadequately alleged. Even with liberal construction, the complaint does not allege the reason that defendants became indebted to the O'Learys other than for the costs incurred by the O'Learys to remediate defendants' assertedly substandard work. While those costs are recoverable as contract damages, they do not support a common count claim. If the O'Leary can do so in good faith, they are given leave to allege a cognizable common count claim. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org. (L&M302)
